heading: Triennial reports of Director of NIH
label: 283 Triennial reports of Director of NIH
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 283, , as added , , ; amended , , , required a biennial report by the Director to the President and Congress, prior to repeal by , , .act July 1, 1944, ch. 373, title IV, § 403Pub. L. 99–158, § 299 Stat. 826Pub. L. 100–607, title I, § 112102 Stat. 3052Pub. L. 109–482, title I, § 104(a)(3)120 Stat. 3689Nov. 20, 1985Nov. 4, 1988Jan. 15, 2007

Amendments2016—, substituted “Triennial” for “Biennial” in section catchline.Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(1)Subsec. (a). , substituted “triennial” for “biennial” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(2)(A)Subsec. (a)(3). , amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “Classification and justification for the priorities established by the agencies, including a strategic plan and recommendations for future research initiatives to be carried out under through the Division of Program Coordination, Planning, and Strategic Initiatives.”Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(2)(B)section 282(b)(7) of this titleSubsec. (a)(4)(B). , substituted “demographic variables, including biological and social variables and relevant age categories (such as pediatric subgroups), and determinants of health,” for “demographic variables and other variables”.Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(2)(C)(i)Subsec. (a)(4)(C)(v). , substituted “demographic variables, including relevant age categories (such as pediatric subgroups), information submitted by each national research institute and national center to the Director of National Institutes of Health under , and such” for “demographic variables and such” and “and other applicable requirements regarding inclusion of demographic groups” for “(regarding inclusion of women and minorities in clinical research)”.Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(2)(C)(ii)section 289a–2(f) of this titleSubsec. (a)(6). , substituted “the following—” for “the following:” in introductory provisions, “an evaluation” for “An evaluation” and “; and” for the period in subpar. (A), redesignated subpar. (C) as (B) and substituted “recommendations” for “Recommendations”, and struck out former subpars. (B) and (D), which read as follows:Pub. L. 114–255, § 2032(2)(D)“(B) Recommendations for promoting coordination of information among the centers of excellence.“(D) If no additional centers of excellence have been funded under this subchapter since the previous report under this section, an explanation of the reasons for not funding any additional centers.”2007—Subsec. (a)(4)(C)(iv)(III). inserted “and postdoctoral training funded through research grants” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 110–85

Effective DateSection applicable only with respect to amounts appropriated for fiscal year 2007 or subsequent fiscal years, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2007 Amendment note under .section 109 of Pub. L. 109–482section 281 of this title

children.text: An assessment of the state of biomedical and behavioral research.
A description of the activities conducted or supported by the agencies of the National Institutes of Health and policies respecting the programs of such agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: identification of the percentage of funds made available by each national research institute and national center with respect to each applicable fiscal year for conducting or supporting research that involves collaboration between the institute or center and 1 or more other national research institutes or national centers; and

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations for promoting coordination of information among the centers of excellence.

children.label: i
children.text: identify the agency or agencies involved;

children.label: ii
children.text: state whether the activity was carried out directly by the agencies or was supported by the agencies and describe to what extent the agency was involved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: identify whether the activity was carried out through a center of excellence.

children.label: B
children.text: In the case of clinical research, the catalog shall, as appropriate, identify study populations by demographic variables, including biological and social variables and relevant age categories (such as pediatric subgroups), and determinants of health, that contribute to research on minority health and health disparities.

children.label: i
children.text: Epidemiological studies and longitudinal studies.

children.label: ii
children.text: Disease registries, information clearinghouses, and other data systems.

children.label: iii
children.text: Public education and information campaigns.

children.label: I
children.text: National Research Service Awards and Clinical Transformation Science Awards;

children.label: II
children.text: graduate medical education programs, including information on the number and type of graduate degrees awarded during the period in which the programs received funding under this subchapter;

children.label: III
children.text: investigator-initiated awards for postdoctoral training and postdoctoral training funded through research grants;

children.label: IV
children.text: a breakdown by demographic variables and other appropriate categories; and

children.label: V
children.text: an evaluation and comparison of outcomes and effectiveness of various training programs.

children.label: v
children.text: Clinical trials, including a breakdown of participation by study populations and demographic variables, including relevant age categories (such as pediatric subgroups), information submitted by each national research institute and national center to the Director of National Institutes of Health under , and such other information as may be necessary to demonstrate compliance with and other applicable requirements regarding inclusion of demographic groups.section 289a–2(f) of this titlesection 289a–2 of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: Translational research activities with other agencies of the Public Health Service.

children.label: A
children.text: Cancer.

children.label: B
children.text: Neurosciences.

children.label: C
children.text: Life stages, human development, and rehabilitation.

children.label: D
children.text: Organ systems.

children.label: E
children.text: Autoimmune diseases.

children.label: F
children.text: Genomics.

children.label: G
children.text: Molecular biology and basic science.

children.label: H
children.text: Technology development.

children.label: I
children.text: Chronic diseases, including pain and palliative care.

children.label: J
children.text: Infectious diseases and bioterrorism.

children.label: K
children.text: Minority health and health disparities.

children.label: L
children.text: Such additional categories as the Director determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: an evaluation of the performance and research outcomes of each center of excellence; and

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations for improving the effectiveness, efficiency, and outcomes of the centers of excellence.

children.heading: Requirement regarding disease-specific research activities
children.label: b Requirement regarding disease-specific research activities

children.text: present information in a standardized format;
identify the actual dollar amounts obligated for such activities; and

include a plan for research on the specific disease, disorder, or other adverse health condition, including a statement of objectives regarding the research, the means for achieving the objectives, a date by which the objectives are expected to be achieved, and justifications for revisions to the plan.

children.heading: Additional reports
children.label: c Additional reports
children.text: In addition to reports required by subsections (a) and (b), the Director of NIH or the head of a national research institute or national center may submit to the Congress such additional reports as the Director or the head of such institute or center determines to be appropriate.
